extraction.one_line_summary: VAN DER POEL won in a sprint against VAN AERT after ALAPHILIPPE crashed into a motorbike while leading, eliminating the strongest attacker.
tactical_patterns: ALAPHILIPPE attacked twice before main climbs started, forcing VAN DER POEL and VAN AERT to chase prematurely inside 50km.
VAN AERT mysteriously dropped through field mid-race but remounted effort to rejoin lead group, revealing exceptional recovery capacity.
VAN DER POEL delayed sprint until 200m despite time advantage, suggesting fear of VAN AERT's longer sustained power.
BETTIOL and NAESEN mentioned as big hitters reacting to early moves, yet absent from final podium selection.

rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: launched two race-splitting attacks before cobbled finale, strongest rider before motorbike collision eliminated him
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: positive: won sprint but needed full distance to barely hold VAN AERT, suggesting equal raw speed
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: survived mid-race crisis and nearly won from compromised position, exceptional diesel engine resilience
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: neutral: contested podium sprint but finished distant third, lacks acceleration against Monument-caliber finishers
rider_signals.TURGIS Anthony: positive: fourth place after contesting sprint with KRISTOFF, punching above expected Monument finishing ability
rider_signals.VAN BAARLE Dylan: neutral: eighth place as expected domestique result, present in finale without podium threat
rider_signals.DEGENKOLB John: neutral: ninth place shows presence in finale but no competitive sprint against top tier
rider_signals.BENOOT Tiesj: neutral: tenth place, survived selection but lacked final acceleration for podium challenge
rider_signals.BETTIOL Alberto: negative: mentioned as big hitter responding to early attacks but absent from final selection
rider_signals.NAESEN Oliver: negative: cited reacting to ALAPHILIPPE's accelerations but failed to reach podium group
